message:
perf(stats): skip recent-activity collection in quiet output mode

`br stats --quiet` (and other Quiet-mode invocations of the stats
command) discard the human-readable rendering, including the recent
activity section. Computing recent_activity still walks the JSONL
audit log and queries the storage layer to bucket events by hour,
which is wasted work when the result will never be displayed.

Adds a new `should_collect_activity` const helper that conjuncts the
existing `should_include_activity` flag with `!Quiet`. The early
OutputContext is constructed up front (with `early=true`) so we can
inspect the resolved output mode before deciding whether to call
`compute_recent_activity`. JSON, JSONL, and the default text/table
modes still collect activity exactly as before; only the explicit
`--quiet` exit path skips the work.

Adds a regression test (`test_should_collect_activity_skips_true_quiet_mode`)
covering the three relevant cases: JSON keeps activity on, Quiet
suppresses it, and `--no-activity` continues to win regardless of
output mode.
